items.title: Trump pours cold water on NATO allies' united front
items.description: Trump criticized NATO allies over defense spending, attacked Spain and renewed his Greenland push during the alliance’s summit in Turkey.

items.title: Trump doubles down on push for control over Greenland as Denmark vows to defend it
items.description: U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday said the U.S. needs to control Greenland “for the protection of the world."

items.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is 'over' after latest round of strikes
items.description: The president was asked about the ceasefire after an escalation of fighting in the Middle East.

items.title: Trump says he doesn't want anything to do with Spain: 'Cut off all trade'
items.description: U.S. President Donald Trump slammed Spain for not contributing enough to NATO, as he attended the defense alliance summit in Turkey.

items.title: OpenAI secures U.S. regulatory green light for GPT-5.6 rollout, Axios report says
SambaNova hits $11 billion valuation as investors back Nvidia chip challengers
items.description: SambaNova is valued at $11 billion after fresh financing led by General Atlantic, as AI chip startups look to challenge Nvidia.

items.title: Singapore's Temasek hits record portfolio value, eyes more investment in AI, infrastructure and private credit
items.description: Over the course of its financial year, Temasek recorded a gain of S$49 billion and a 10.5% total shareholder return.

items.title: China warns about AI risks with Anthropic's Claude Code
items.description: China said specific versions of Claude Code posed back-door vulnerabilities that could send sensitive information to a remote server.

items.title: Samsung-backed AI chip firm Rebellions targets IPO in South Korea next year, CEO tells CNBC
items.description: Sunghyun Park, CEO of Rebellions, told CNBC that the company is leaning toward a listing on the KOSPI over the KOSDAQ.

items.title: This former Apple executive is betting on Shenzhen, not Silicon Valley, to create the 'next Apple'
items.description: Startups building consumer electronics will have a better shot at becoming the next Apple in Shenzhen than in Silicon Valley, said Will Wang, CEO of China's smart-glasses startup.

items.title: Andy Burnham's theory of devolution for Britain
items.description: The U.K.'s prime minister in waiting wants to devolve power from London to the nation’s regions. But past efforts have been anything but successful.

items.title: Apple begins testing CXMT chips for devices sold in China, FT says
Lawmakers probe growing use of Chinese AI models in U.S. companies
items.description: An ongoing House Committee investigation is probing the risks involved in the rise of AI built in China.

items.title: Chinese autonomous-driving firm Momenta rises 3% in Hong Kong stock debut
items.description: Chinese autonomous driving firm Momenta debuted on the Hong Kong stock exchange on Wednesday, rising 3%.

items.title: China's rare missile test will push wary Asia-Pacific countries to close ranks, analysts say
items.description: China's rare launch of a ballistic missile launch into the Pacific will push regional powers to deepen their defense ties.

items.title: Netflix, Disney and YouTube interested in FIFA World Cup U.S. rights; package could reach $2 billion
items.description: FIFA has alerted media companies that English- and Spanish-language U.S. rights are likely to be sold together for 2030 and 2034, likely driving up the price.

items.title: Kalshi traders see slim odds U.S. government will take a stake in OpenAI this year
items.description: Traders on the prediction markets platform see a less than 30% likelihood the federal government will take a stake in AI companies Anthropic and OpenAI.

items.title: Jim Cramer says the market's reaction to Samsung may signal a shift in AI leadership
items.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said Samsung's earnings sparked a sell-off in AI hardware stocks, while many tech names that have lagged this year rebounded.

items.title: Judge blocks DOJ subpoena for names of 2020 Fulton County, Georgia, election workers
items.description: President Donald Trump has focused on the ballot count in Fulton County, Georgia, to promote claims that he actually won the 2020 election

items.title: U.S. revokes Iran oil sales authorization after tanker attacks
items.description: Treasury had waived sanctions on Iranian oil through Aug. 21 after Washington and Tehran inked an interim deal to reopen Hormuz last month.
